description:
## What

`main`'s sig is `(frame_ptr: i64, host_ctx: i64) -> i64 system_v` because every uniform fn goes through the trampoline (`run_internal` at src/ir_codegen.rs:233 allocates a frame and dispatches via transmute). Post-.27.18, `main`'s body never references `frame_ptr` (v0) — but the sig still declares it because `build_fn_signature` hardcodes the uniform shape.

Lift the explicit exclusion at `parking.rs:127` (`if Some(f.id) == main_id { continue; }`) and change `run_internal` (and `run_quantum`, AOT entry wrapper) to call main natively when the analysis admits it. Main becomes `(host_ctx: i64) -> i64 tail` — no frame_ptr, no trampoline loop for the top frame.

## Why this is tractable

- main is never a cont target / never called from fz IR (`.27.18` analysis confirms this).
- main's body, after `.27.18`, doesn't read frame_ptr — there's no semantic dependency.
- main's return value becomes the halt value directly; the host captures it and calls `fz_halt` itself (or passes to Process's halt_slot).

## Codegen + harness changes

1. Lift the main exclusion in `parking.rs:127`. The fixed point now admits main iff its body satisfies the same body_ok invariants as any other native fn.
2. `run_internal` branches on `natively_callable.contains(&main_id)`:
   - Native: skip frame alloc, transmute to `extern "C" fn(host_ctx: i64) -> i64`, call directly, return.
   - Uniform: existing trampoline loop unchanged.
3. `run_quantum`: same branching, but only on the first invocation (the entry frame). Subsequent quanta continue the trampoline normally.
4. AOT entry wrapper: same dispatch in the C-callable `main` symbol.
5. Native main might be reached with Term::Halt or Term::Return as the terminator:
   - `Term::Return(v)`: `b.ins().return_(&[coerced_v])`. Existing native-Return path.
   - `Term::Halt(v)`: existing native-Halt path (line 2719+) which already handles it: call fz_halt; return val. Compatible.

## Trickiness

- Native main's return repr depends on its return Descr. For `fn main()` returning `print(...)` (Nil bits = 3), repr is Tagged. Host harness reads the i64 and interprets — same as today's halt_slot value semantically.
- If main has a body that today triggers Term::Halt (explicit halt), the native-Halt path handles it. fz_halt's halt_slot write still happens; return val is the halt value.
- Calling convention: the typed-native ABI uses `CallConv::Tail`. From Rust, calling a Tail-conv fn via `extern "C"` is unsupported. Either:
  - Emit main with `CallConv::SystemV` as a special case (entry fn doesn't need Tail because nothing tail-calls into it).
  - Add a SystemV `main_entry` adapter that calls main via Tail conv and returns.
  
  Pick the simpler first option. `build_fn_signature` learns about an `is_entry: bool` for the SystemV override.

## Tests (TDD)

1. CLIF assertion on fixtures/add1.fz: `main` sig is `(i64) -> i64 system_v` (just host_ctx) — no frame_ptr. `block0(v0: i64):` — single param.
2. Behavioural: add1.fz still prints 42 via JIT and AOT.
3. Spec parity + fixture matrix all green.
4. Verify recursive / cont-heavy fixtures (fact, map_higher_order) still trampoline normally — they don't have native main, so the dispatch branch falls through.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- main CLIF: single-param block0, no `v0+16` load anywhere.
- No regression in tests that invoke non-main entry fns via `run(other_fn_id)`.

## Blocks

Blocked by .27.19 — main's sig dropping frame_ptr means simultaneously trimming host_ctx if the analysis says main doesn't need it. Or, more precisely: this ticket ALSO depends on the per-fn-sig-trim infrastructure, so doing .27.19 first means main's native sig naturally arrives at the right shape.

## Out of scope

- Generalizing "make any only-an-entry uniform fn native." Just main for now; tests rarely use other fns as entry with this optimization.
title: Make main native — bypass trampoline for the entry fn
